title: Meta stock sinks after Q1 earnings as company raises 2026 AI spending forecast to $125 billion-$145 billion
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Meta stock dropped 8% after Q1 earnings as the company raised its 2026 AI spending forecast to $125-$145 billion, exceeding previous estimates. Despite strong e
excerpt: Meta stock dropped 8% after Q1 earnings as the company raised its 2026 AI spending forecast to $125-$145 billion, exceeding previous estimates. Despite strong earnings, concerns over high capital expenditures impacted investor sentiment.

content.introduction: Meta stock dropped 8% after Q1 earnings as the company raised its 2026 AI spending forecast to $125-$145 billion, exceeding previous estimates. Despite strong earnings, concerns over high capital expenditures impacted investor sentiment.
body: Meta stock sinks as its AI spending forecast shoots up to $145 billion. The company beat revenue estimates and posted strong profit growth, but

*   [**IBD 50**](https://research.investors.com/stock-lists/ibd-50/). *   [Stock Lists Update](https://www.investors.com/stock-lists/best-growth-stocks-buy-watch-ibd-stock-lists/). *   [Stock Of The Day](https://www.investors.com/research/ibd-stock-of-the-day/). *   [IBD Stock Analysis](https://www.investors.com/category/research/ibd-stock-analysis/). *   [Screen Of The Day](https://www.investors.com/research/best-stocks-to-buy-watch-ibd-screen-of-the-day/). *   [Earnings Calendar](https://www.investors.com/research/earnings-calendar-analyst-estimates-stocks-to-watch/). *   [IBD 50 Stocks To Watch](https://www.investors.com/research/ibd-50-growth-stocks-to-watch/). *   [IBD Data Stories](https://www.investors.com/ibd-data-stories/build-your-investing-watch-list-with-stock-ratings-buy-zones-earnings/). *   [Educational Resources](https://www.investors.com/news/technology/meta-stock-q1-2026-earnings/). Zuckerberg [told analysts](https://www.investors.com/news/technology/meta-stock-q4-ai-spending/) in January that the company expects to spend $115 billion to $135 billion on 2026 capital expenditures. That's after spending about $70 billion in 2025, a total that was [revised higher by Meta](https://www.investors.com/news/technology/meta-stock-q3-2025-earnings-ai-meta-news-zuckerberg/) throughout the year. On the [stock market today](https://www.investors.com/news/stock-market-today-stock-market-news/?), Meta stock dropped more than 7% to 616 premarket. *   ##### [Stock Market Today: Dow Adds 700 Points; Meta Falls, Lilly Up 10% As AI Data Centers Lead Gains (Live Coverage)](https://www.investors.com/market-trend/stock-market-today/dow-jones-sp500-nasdaq-inflation-data-meta-stock-meta-earnings/). *   ##### [Meta Stock Edges Lower With Q1 Results Due. We use cookies to improve your experience and for marketing. Analysts had projected earnings of $6.65 per share on revenue of $55.5 billion, but Meta reported earnings of $10.44 per share on sales of $56.3 billion. However, a closer examination reveals that a significant portion of the earnings growth was attributable to a $5 billion tax benefit, which is not a recurring source of income that investors can expect to rely upon. Additionally, Meta's operating profit rose by only 30%, a slower pace than its sales growth, as the company's expenses increased at a rate exceeding that of its revenue. The company allocated $19 billion of the $32.2 billion in cash generated from operations during the first quarter to capital expenditures for expanding its AI business. This left Meta with $13.2 billion in free cash flow, which is less than half of the $26.8 billion the company reported as earnings for the quarter. This suggests that AI spending is diminishing the quality of Meta's earnings.
